prompt: Generate a network graph showing connections between two readers and four books. Use Image 1 (Person A) and Image 2 (Person B) as nodes for the readers, and Image 3 to Image 6 as nodes for the books. Draw lines between each person and the books they have read (Person A read Book 1, 3, and 4; Person B read Book 2 and 3)
prompt_refined: Generate a clear and organized network graph on a plain white background. Position the two reader nodes on the left side of the graph: the female reader from Image 1 at the top-left, and the male reader from Image 2 at the bottom-left. Position the four book nodes on the right side of the graph: Image 3 (Anna Karenina) and Image 4 (Harry Potter) in the top-right quadrant, and Image 5 (To Kill a Mockingbird) and Image 6 (Oliver Twist) in the bottom-right quadrant. Draw distinct lines to represent reading connections: connect Image 1 (female reader) to Image 3 (Anna Karenina), Image 5 (To Kill a Mockingbird), and Image 6 (Oliver Twist). Connect Image 2 (male reader) to Image 4 (Harry Potter) and Image 5 (To Kill a Mockingbird).
objects: female reader node from Image 1 positioned at the top-left
male reader node from Image 2 positioned at the bottom-left
Anna Karenina book node from Image 3 in the top-right quadrant
Harry Potter book node from Image 4 in the top-right quadrant
To Kill a Mockingbird book node from Image 5 in the bottom-right quadrant
Oliver Twist book node from Image 6 in the bottom-right quadrant
distinct lines connecting female reader node to Anna Karenina, To Kill a Mockingbird, and Oliver Twist book nodes
distinct lines connecting male reader node to Harry Potter and To Kill a Mockingbird book nodes
